channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Politics Friday: Minnesota’s paid leave law remains a flashpoint as launch nears
item.description: MPR News senior politics editor Brian Bakst talks with two Minnesota lawmakers about the state’s new paid leave program — scheduled to start in January of next year — and the legislation that could possibly delay or change eligibility.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: DHS revokes visa of one Columbia student, arrests another
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) announced Friday it has revoked the visa of one Columbia University student and arrested another, escalating its crackdown on students engaged in protests over the conflict in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic Party fractures in government shutdown fight, with anger running high
item.description: The Democratic Party appears fractured as a torrent of frustration and anger has been unleashed on Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Matt Brown And Steve Peoples, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nevada senators split on vote ahead of potential government shutdown
item.description: Nevada's two Democratic senators split their decisions Friday ahead of a vote in the U.S. Senate to avoid a potential partial federal government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOD closes think tank arm marred by 'inefficiency' and criticized by GOP for ties to Trump-Russia probe
item.description: The Department of Defense has dissolved its Office of Net Acquisition, an office that was meant to provide strategic analysis but allegedly got caught up in the Trump-Russia investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In Michigan, Vance says US manufacturing can rebound despite tariff jitters and falling markets
item.description: Vice President JD Vance has toured a Michigan plastics manufacturing facility while promoting a promised Trump administration industrial renaissance nationwide even as jitters about rising tariffs and steep drops in consumer confidence and financial markets point to the opposite.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti And Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Musk meets with head of National Security Agency to ensure it is aligned with Trump, spy agency says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk met this week with the leader of the National Security Agency, suggesting the nation's spy services won't escape the billionaire's scrutiny as he looks to slash spending and personnel across the federal government. Musk met Wednesday with Gen.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former US Sen. Alan Simpson of Wyoming, who bridged partisan gaps with his quick wit, dies at age 93
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — Former U.S. Sen. Alan Simpson, a political legend whose quick wit bridged partisan gaps in the years before today’s political acrimony, has died. He was 93.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE lease cancellations: These government offices could be closed in Minnesota
item.description: The General Services Administration, working with Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, has notified landlords that federal agencies will be terminating hundreds of leases for offices and buildings in the coming months — including a dozen in Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: VP Vance jeered at Kennedy Center concert: video
item.description: Richard Grenell blasted leftists as "intolerant" after jeers erupted when Vice President JD Vance attended a Kennedy Center concert.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats set to host town halls targeting vulnerable House Republicans
item.description: Democratic committees are set to host town halls across the country to target vulnerable House Republicans ahead of the 2026 midterms.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Immigration officials arrest second person who participated in pro-Palestinian protests at Columbia
item.description: Immigration officials have arrested a second person who participated in Pro-Palestinian protests at Columbia University, and have revoked the visa of another student.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democratic Rep. Raúl Grijalva of Arizona, champion of environment and progressivism, dies at age 77
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democratic Rep. Raúl M. Grijalva, a champion of environmental protections and progressive ideals who took on principled but often futile causes during a two-decade career in Congress, died Thursday. Grijalva, who was 77, had risen to chair the U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES and LEAH ASKARINAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats signal plans to advance GOP bill to avoid a government shutdown
item.description: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, D-NY, said he plans to vote for a Republican bill to fund the government through September, paving the way for other Democrats to join him.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh, Claudia Grisales, and Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Putin will always be a threat to stability in Europe: Dissident
item.description: Ukrainian leaders have already agreed to a 30-day halt in fighting, and now, U.S. diplomats are seeking Russia’s acceptance.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: California bill that would make it illegal to defend yourself pulled
item.description: Assembly Bill 1333, which would have changed what qualifies as self-defense and eliminated circumstances such as defending your home or property, has been pulled due to public pressure and criticism from District Attorneys.
item.dc:creator: Dom McAndrew, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration tariffs create uncertainty for brewery owner
item.description: Cary said Begyle Brewing imports about 200K pounds of barley from Canada each year and that tariffs would hurt the brewery's margins.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: White House withdraws nomination of David Weldon to run the CDC
item.description: Dr. Dave Weldon, Trump's pick for director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, was withdrawn from consideration shortly before a scheduled Senate confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: Activist Mahmoud Khalil's arrest 'deeply concerning': Ex-NYC mayor
item.description: "You don't get arrested for disagreeing with the U.S. government," said former NYC Mayor Bill de Blasio.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Trump meets with NATO chief Mark Rutte
item.description: The meeting comes after Trump called on European allies to contribute more to the alliance's defense fund.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: USDA ends programs that bolster school lunches, food banks
item.description: A USDA spokesperson confirms the programs are being terminated because they “no longer effectuate the goals of the agency.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota House is tied after DFL wins special election. Here’s what changes
item.description: DFLer David Gottfried won the suburban Ramsey County seat and is expected to be sworn in to the House of Representatives next week.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump vows to take back 'stolen' wealth as tariffs on steel and aluminum imports go into effect
item.description: President Donald Trump is openly challenging U.S. allies by increasing tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports to 25 percent as he vows to take back wealth he says was “stolen” by other countries, drawing quick retaliation from Europe and Canada.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump says tariffs will be 'positive'; Vance pushes Russia for ceasefire
item.description: “The United States of America is going to take back a lot of what was stolen from it by other countries.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Jeanne Shaheen to retire, setting up battle for New Hampshire Senate seat
item.description: “It was a difficult decision, made more difficult by the current environment in the country — by President Trump and what he’s doing right now,” said Shaheen, who is 78.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Judge dismisses lawsuit claiming tribal casinos in Minnesota are running illegal games
item.description: A federal district court judge has dismissed a lawsuit brought by one of Minnesota’s horse racing tracks against executives who oversee five tribal casinos in the state.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: Federal judge cancels hearing in NYC Mayor Eric Adams' corruption case
item.description: MANHATTAN, N.Y. (PIX11) – A hearing in the corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams has been canceled for Friday. U.S. District Judge Dale Ho, who's weighing the Justice Department's request to dismiss the charges against Adams, called the hearing off.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Overpaid by Social Security? You’ll now owe 100% of the repayment
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – If you accidentally got overpaid through your Social Security benefits, you’ll have to pay 100% of it back starting in late March, the agency announced on Monday. The overpayment withholding rate was previously 10%.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says he’s buying ‘brand new’ Tesla to support Musk
item.description: President Donald Trump said he would be buying a “brand new” Tesla to support his senior adviser Elon Musk, as the stock market shows shares of the electric car company tumbling.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5
